frameworks.name: Bayesian structural inference
frameworks.one_line: observer infers latent motion-relation structure probabilistically from ambiguous visual scenes

frameworks.name: Bayesian ideal observer
frameworks.one_line: task-specific normative model serving as choice-model backbone for human comparison

frameworks.name: hierarchical generative model of motion
frameworks.one_line: object motion nested within moving reference frames as latent structure

core_question: How do humans identify the latent relational structure underlying motion in a visual scene?
key_assumptions: scene motion is generated by latent hierarchical structures
observers perform probabilistic inference over these structures
manipulations: hierarchical nesting of object motion within reference frames
task design exposing structural-inference signatures across trials
key_findings: human structural-inference performance tracks Bayesian ideal observer
perceptual error patterns match probabilistic-reasoning predictions
single-trial responses and individual differences are captured by the choice model
subjective confidence aligns with model especially under ambiguity and nesting
interpretations: motion perception exhibits hallmarks of Bayesian structural inference
probabilistic reasoning over latent structure underlies relational motion perception
